description: AWS-specific rules — apply ONLY to nodes where cloud_provider is AWS

rules.title: Security Group Exposes EC2 to Internet
rules.message: [NODE_ID] is a Security Group with 0.0.0.0/0 open that EXPOSES a COMPUTE node — AWS-SG-001 violated. Direct internet attack surface exists. Source: AWS CIS Benchmark v1.4 Rule 4.1.
rules.fix: Remove 0.0.0.0/0 inbound rule from {node_id}. Allow only specific CIDR ranges. This immediately breaks the attack path entry point.
judge_scenarios: What is my biggest AWS security risk?
Are my EC2 instances exposed to internet?
What is the entry point of the attack path?
Which AWS resources are critically vulnerable?
Does my AWS setup follow CIS Benchmark?

rules.title: Privilege Escalation Path Between IAM Roles
rules.message: [NODE_ID] has a PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION edge to an admin IAM role — AWS-IAM-001 violated. ReadOnly role can reach Admin role. This is the most critical AWS IAM finding. Source: AWS IAM Access Analyzer.
rules.fix: Remove the PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION relationship between IAM roles immediately. A ReadOnly role must never have a path to an Admin role. Review all role trust policies and permission boundaries.
judge_scenarios: Can an attacker escalate privileges in my AWS setup?
Is my IAM architecture safe?
What is the privilege escalation risk?
How can an attacker reach my database?
What are my critical AWS vulnerabilities?

rules.title: EC2 Instance Has Overprivileged IAM
rules.message: [NODE_ID] is an EC2 instance with IAM_OVERPRIVILEGED flag — AWS-EC2-001 violated. The IAM role attached to this instance has more permissions than needed. Source: AWS Well-Architected Framework Security Pillar.
rules.fix: Review and restrict the IAM role attached to {node_id}. Follow least privilege principle. Use IAM Access Analyzer to identify unused permissions. Remove all permissions that are not actively used.
judge_scenarios: Is my EC2 instance secure?
Which EC2 instances have IAM issues?
What permissions does my VM have?
Does my AWS setup follow Well-Architected Framework?
What are my IAM violations in AWS?

rules.title: RDS Database Missing Encryption or Backup
rules.message: [NODE_ID] is an AWS RDS instance with encryption or backup disabled — AWS-RDS-001 violated. Production databases must have both encryption and automated backup enabled. Source: AWS RDS Security Best Practices.
rules.fix: Enable both encryption at rest AND automated backups on {node_id}. For encryption: create encrypted snapshot and restore. For backup: set backup retention period to minimum 7 days in RDS settings.
judge_scenarios: Is my AWS database secure?
Does my RDS follow best practices?
What happens if my AWS database is compromised?
Which AWS resources violate SOC2?
Is my production data protected?

rules.title: Complete Attack Path from Internet to Database
rules.message: CRITICAL ATTACK PATH DETECTED — AWS-ATTACK-001. Internet can reach your production DATABASE through a 5-hop chain: SECURITY_GROUP → COMPUTE → IAM_ROLE → IAM_ROLE(escalated) → DATABASE. This is the highest severity finding. Source: AWS Threat Detection Framework.
rules.fix: Break this attack chain at the earliest point: 1) Close 0.0.0.0/0 on security group. 2) Remove PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION edge between IAM roles. 3) Revoke FullAccess from admin IAM role. ANY ONE of these breaks the path.
judge_scenarios: What is my biggest security threat?
Can someone reach my database from internet?
What is the complete attack path?
How many hops to compromise my database?
What is the blast radius of an attack?
